summary: Postinstall registers privileged Windows SYSTEM service that accepts and executes remote commands from author-controlled IM endpoint, with unpinned 6-hour auto-update
details: On `npm install` under a Windows Administrator shell, scripts/post-install.js silently uses node-windows to register a SYSTEM-level service named 'claw-subagent-service' pointing at service/daemon.js, configures it via `sc.exe` to auto-start at boot and to restart on failure (`reset= 0 actions= restart/0/restart/0/restart/0`), and starts it. The service forks service/worker.js, which connects to a hardcoded author-operated RongCloud IM endpoint (default `https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im`, with `/api/claw/token/<nodeId>` for token issuance) and processes inbound messages whose `msg_type` is routed through CommandHandler/ScriptExecutor. service/modules/script-executor.js's `executeCommandDirect` spawns `bash -c <command>` (and packaged.bat/.sh) using content delivered over the IM channel, giving the operator arbitrary code execution as SYSTEM on every installer host. service/rongcloud/openclaw-client.js further passes remote-supplied `escapedMessage` and `sessionId` strings into `spawn(command, { shell: true })` with an escape routine that handles only `\`, `"`, and CRLF — backticks and `$()` are not escaped, providing an additional command-injection sink reachable from the same channel. service/updater.js schedules `setInterval(run, 6h)` to call `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<latest>` via execAsync with no version pin or signature verification, so any future publisher push (or publisher-account compromise) is auto-installed by the SYSTEM service and re-runs postinstall. Installer impact: persistent SYSTEM-level remote-controlled agent + automatic re-install of future versions, established by a single `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
